title: Ouratea finlayi
intro: Ouratea finlayi, also known as the "South American passionflower," is a plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ouratea finlayi include its ability to act as an anxiolytic and sedative, making it useful for treating anxiety and insomnia, as well as its potential to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. Bioactive constituents of Ouratea finlayi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Ouratea finlayi is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants like passionflower (Passiflora incarnata) to enhance its anxiolytic and sedative effects.
taxonomy: Ouratea finlayi is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. The plant is native to New Caledonia. Ouratea finlayi belongs to the genus Ouratea, which is a group of flowering plants that are mostly found in tropical regions. The species is characterized by its unique features and is closely related to other species within the Ochnaceae family. Ouratea finlayi is an important part of the region's biodiversity and is studied by botanists for its distinct characteristics.
